Jamrs A. Mcdonnell, one Of the bes


known railroads contractors in the Can
adam west, and the owner of the town


site of Morrisey, b. c., is in the city
on his way north from trip to Of


tawa and Montreal. He has been in
the east investigating the possibility
of securing contracts on the Grand
Trunk-Paciic railway, Canada's new
national highway. Seen this morn


ing at the Hotel Spokane he said:
CONTRACTS will be let early in the
spring for construction work to be
started simultaneously at several places
on the line of the proposed Grand
Trunk Pacific railway. The dominion
government have guaranteed the inter


est on TIE bonds for the construct "
of the road. This is the only subsidy
to be given, and there is no land grant
bonus similar to what the Canadian
